entry.title: SAP Best Practices for SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain – 2405
entry.content:
Welcome to our blog on the release highlights of SAP Best Practices for SAP Integrated Business Planning 2405. In this release, we've introduced several enhancements and new features that will greatly benefit you. Let's dive into the details.

Sample Planning Area SAPIBP1: 

The SAPIBP1 sample planning area has undergone some exciting enhancements.

  • We've added slow-moving indicator key figures, which are calculated by dividing the total future consumption quantity by the initial stock quantity. With the slow-Moving Indicator key figure, you can monitor and make time-dependent investigations of the slow or non-moving products in your stock. 
    This functionality will provide valuable insights into slow-moving items and help planners make informed decisions.
  • We have also simplified the STDDEVACTUALSQTY calculation using the IBP_ADD_TIME_DIMENSION function. 
  • We've added a new key figure called FOOTPRINTSUPPLIERPERUNIT, which captures the footprint, for example for a raw material per supplier and plant.
  • We've enhanced the BestFit forecast models with the Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGB) forecast algorithm. XGB is an ensemble machine learning algorithm that allows demand planners to make better predictions and decisions, for example, in forecasting sales or identifying problem areas in inventory or delivery.

You can find all changes on sample planning area SAPIBP1 and the details behind them here. 

Inventory Optimization

Moving on to the solution process for IBP inventory (11Z), we've made some important improvements.

  • Supply lead time prediction has been added, providing users with a better understanding into actual lead times and enabling them to optimize their inventory management.
  • We have also introduced a preconfigured analytics story specifically designed for inventory planners. This story will provide them with comprehensive analytics and visualizations to make informed decisions. It helps the planner to validate inputs for inventory operators, to analyze results after running inventory operators and to review final inventory plans. 
    To support theses processes, the following pages are available in the story: 
    • Overview, which allows you to review results of inventory planning runs, such as the costs of safety stock and distribution. You can also use filters on this page to focus these metrics on particular location products.
    • Inventory Health, which allows you to identify slow-moving stock at location products, as well as to group and compare stock based on consumption rate
    • Safety Stock Drivers, which allows you to review the amount of safety stock allocated to location products to cover variability in demand, service, and supply.
    • Forecast Accuracy, which allows you to evaluate forecasting accuracy, demand variability, and the safety stock for covering forecast uncertainty.
    • Lead Time Quality, which allows you to evaluate production and transportation lead times, lead time variability, and parameters for reviewing and replenishing stock.
    • Scenario Analysis, which allows you to run scenarios and simulations for evaluating how adjustments to target service levels impact safety stock.
    • Inventory Leverage, which allows you to run scenarios and simulations for evaluating how changes to inventory holding cost rate impact safety stock costs and service levels.
    • Safety Stock Review, which allows you to review and compare how safety stock recommendations at location products have changed between the previous planning run and the current planning run.

Here some screenshots: 

Overview

 

Leadtime

 

Drivers

 

You can also check this document to learn more about this analytics story for inventory planner, but also about the other preconfigured analytics stories. 

  • Another enhancement in the IBP for inventory solution process is the inclusion of a slow-moving indicator. This will help users identify slow-moving items and take appropriate actions to optimize their inventory.

In the solution process IBP inventory - scenario planning (4YI), we have introduced an additional scenario to simulate the impact of adjusting the inventory holding cost rate. This will enable users to evaluate different cost scenarios and make the right decisions regarding inventory management.

Extended Planning and Analysis (xP&A)

We are also excited to introduce a new document xP&A tips and tricks. This document provides detailed information on model enhancements, configurations, and business process steps required to run Commercial Planning and Consensus Net Revenue Planning integrated with SAP Integrated Business Planning. The process leverages SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP Integrated Business Planning, SAP S/4HANA, and SAP Cloud Integration. This holistic approach allows multiple lines of business, including Finance, Commercial, and Supply Chain, to collaborate effectively.

Integration SAP Integrated Business Planning and SAP Analytics Cloud

 

You can find more information about the related SAP Analytics Cloud Content here: 

  • Commercial Planning
  • Consensus Net Revenue Planning

Transportation Load Building

Lastly, the IBP for response and supply – transportation load building solution process has been enhanced with two additional workbooks in the Planner Workspace. These workbooks, Deployment Loads from Plant to Plant and Purchase Loads from Supplier to Plant, provide users with comprehensive insights into transportation load building, enabling them to optimize their supply chain operations.

Planner Workspace for Transportation Load Building

 

Here some more useful links: 

  • SAP Signavio Process Navigator to download all assets of SAP Best Practices for SAP Integrated Business Planning
  • In case you missed our last webinar about S/4 HANA Cloud, public edition integration and preconfigured analytics stories you can check out here the recording.

Thank you for reading our release highlights blog. Stay tuned for more updates and enhancements in future releases.

entry.title: How to add or integrate S/4 on premise in to Cloud ALM
entry.content:
This document contains all the steps that are needed to add S/4HANA on-premise to Cloud ALM and activate all the required use cases.

Prerequisites:

  1. SAP_BASIS Release: Either 7.40 SP16 or higher (accordingly 7.50 SP05) Or 7.40 SP09 - SP15 (7.50 SP00 - 7.50 SP04) with SAP Note 2283880 - Logon Username not used in RFC API
  2. SAP_UI Version: Either SAP_UI 740 SP15 or higher
  3. Implement ST-PI 7.40 Support Packages and keep them up to date, including the collective corrections suited for your ST-PI support package from the required SAP Notes mentioned below. For instance, make sure to operate your ABAP managed system with at minimum the latest or second latest Support Package, as available on the SAP Support Portal.
  4. Check that profile parameter icm/HTTPS/client_sni_enabled is set to TRUE (see also SAP Note 510007 - Additional considerations for setting up SSL on Application Server ABAP)
  5. Check that profile parameter ssl/client_ciphersuites is set as described in section 7 of SAP Note 510007 - Additional considerations for setting up SSL on Application Server ABAP.
  6. Check that DigiCert Global Root CA is imported in STRUST under SSL Client (Anonymous) and SSL Client (Standard).
  7. Check that DigiCert Global Root G2 is imported in STRUST under SSL Client (Anonymous) and SSL Client (Standard).
  8. Check that DigiCert RSA4096 Root G5 is imported in STRUST under SSL Client (Anonymous) and SSL Client (Standard).

Integration Steps:

We need to download the Cloud ALM JSON file or Service key. This key is needed as Some of the managed SAP Cloud services use a PUSH mechanism to send alerts and exceptions to SAP Cloud ALM need it while adding system.

Go to SAP BTP https://account.hana.ondemand.com/cockpit/ and click as highlighted.

 

Select SAP Cloud ALM and click continue

 

Click on SAP Cloud ALM tenant.

 

 

 

Then click on JSON tab and download the file.
Go to STRUST and right click SSL Client SSL Client (Anonymous) and Create

 

Ensure entries are shown as below and Click ok

 

Download the Digicert certificates from https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/setup-managed-services/setup-abap/setup-strust.html?anchorId=section_1414159140 and import the certs to both the SSL Client (Anonymous) as well as the SSL Client (Standard) in the transaction STRUST.

 

Scroll to the bottom and click Import Certificate.

 

Pass the certificate that we downloaded and click OK.

Once the certificates are uploaded click on Add to Certificate List and Save. Perform the same for other 2 certificates we downloaded.

 

 

 

 

 

 

In S/4HANA go to transaction /n/SDF/ALM_SETUP

Enter the target name and hit enter then next fields will be enabled.

 

Click on Create/Update destination.

 

Copy the contents from downloaded JSON file and paste them. Click Green tick after we pasted the JSON entries.

 

Click on Green Tick as shown below.

 

Enter batch user.

 

 

Click on Choose Use Cases from Step 4

 

Select the use cases that you need.

 

Create an RFC to push data to Cloud ALM.

SM59 à RFC Type G

SLD_DS_TO_SAP_SUPPORT_BACKBONE

 

 

Enter SAP Technical S-user and password.

 

 

Save RFC and ensure connection test is fine.

 

Go to RZ70 and add Cloud ALM entry to push data to Cloud ALM.

Remove the entries from GW hostname and GW Service. Click on Change.

 

 

Enter the RFC we created and select checkbox Active and Save Settings.

Click YES

 

 

 

Validation:

To validate the system in Cloud ALM Go to cloud ALM tenant.

Click on Landscape Management

 

 

 

Additional References:

https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/setup-managed-services/setup-abap-onprem.html?anchorId=section_copy_copy

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003111953

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003367724

entry.title: Re: SAP SD - Organizational Structure
entry.content:
You asked this question already two weeks ago and the answer given back then still hold:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/sales-organizational-structure-ad-service-industry/qaq-p/13678546

This is something which needs to be determined by Finance/Legal as this is depending on VAT regsitrations, presence in countries, business model (f.e. agent/reseller), reporting requirements, etc.

In any way, an invoice can have only one pricing schema (VBRK-KALSM) - so in case you want to have all different kind of services/products billed together on one invoice, you need to incorporate all the various pricing requirements in one pricing procedure.

entry.title: New Card Upload Process and Role-based Filtering of Cards in Work Zone Adv
entry.content:
Background

From SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition U2404 Release, Cards are now supported as a visualization type of local apps and are maintained in the Content Manager. 

It is no longer possible to manually upload standalone cards or update cards through the Admin Console. Remaining functionality in the Admin Console is configuring the card and enabling/disabling the card.

  • It is no longer possible to directly deploy a card created via the Development Tools for SAP Build Work Zone extension in SAP Business Application Studio to the subaccount.

Card Management

1. Go to Content Manager 

With the admin access, you can go to Work Zone Adv system -> Admin Console. Go to the Business Content Tab -> Open Content Manager.

2. Upload Card

You can package and download the card zip from the Development Tools for SAP Build Work Zone extension in SAP Business Application Studio. 
In Content Manager, Click Create -> App

In App Creation, directly go to Visulization Tab, Choose Visualization Type as "UI Integration Card" and Upload Card zip.

3. Setup Role Filtering

3.1 Make Card Available to All Users

To make this card available to all users, you need to assign Everyone role to the app.
In Content Manager -> Find out Everyone Role -> Edit -> Toggel On your app.

        
3.2 Make Card Available to subset Users

To make this card available to a subset of users, you need to create your own role, assign your app to your role, assign your role to the Work Zone site and assign your role to the requied users in the BTP Subaccount.
In Content Manager -> Create -> Role -> Toggel On your app.

      .     
Go to Site Directory -> Open Settings for "SAP Build Work Zone" 
       
 Edit the Site -> Add your role to the site assignments

Go to BTP Subaccount -> Role Collections -> Find out your role -> Configure Users or User Groups to your role who should see your App/Card.

 

4.Enable Card

Before the end user can see or use the card. One last step is to enable the card in Admin Console.
Go to Work Zone Admin Console -> UI Integration -> Cards, Find out your card -> Toggel On the card

 

5. Use Card

The end user go to Work Zone Adv system, go to any Workspaces and edit any Workpage.
If the end user have the role, he can see this card in widget picker and also see the card the workpage.

 

If the end user do not have the role, he should not see the card in the widget picker and workpages.

Tips for Admins

When the admin go to Content Manager/Admin Console, he can see all the apps/cards regardless of his own roles.
When the admin go to Home or any workpages, he will behave like a normal user representing himself, thus he can only see the cards based on this own role.

entry.title: Re: How to reset sample determination in S/4HANA Public C...
entry.content:
Hi, 

Please refer to this Help Page to reset sample determination in Manage Inspection Lots app:

  1. Open the Manage Inspection Lots app.
